methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $8M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Pale Blue is a Series A space and aerospace company founded by Jun Asakawa and Yuichi Nakagawa that has raised $7.5M in equity plus $27M in grants. The target operates within a competitive landscape anchored by 5 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs.

Only the stage-progression method is applicable to this valuation. Using the $8M total raised as a proxy for current implied value, a 2.0x step-up multiplier—typical for Series A to next-stage progression—yields $15M. Private peer comparables could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or valuation-tier datapoints within the narrow Space & Aerospace cohort (2 adjacent-stage peers identified, below the 3-minimum threshold). Public comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 25.8x across 6 tickers) cannot be deployed without Pale Blue's revenue figures, which remain undisclosed.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $13M to $17M, with a median of $15M. This estimate carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single method and the absence of comparable transaction data at Pale Blue's stage and sub-sector. Key unknowns include the company's revenue run-rate, gross margins, and technical differentiation relative to private peers. A revenue disclosure or follow-on funding event would substantially narrow this band.
